name: Pollution of Air and Water
subject: chemistry
summary: Air gets polluted by CO₂, CO, CFCs, and particulates. Water by sewage, fertilisers, and industrial waste. Chemical reactions drive acid rain and eutrophication.

crushed.title: Air pollutants
crushed.text: CO₂ (global warming), CO (poisonous), SO₂ & NO₂ (acid rain), CFCs (ozone hole), smoke particulates.

crushed.title: Acid rain
crushed.text: SO₂ + H₂O → H₂SO₃. NO₂ + H₂O → HNO₃. Damages buildings, kills fish, harms forests.

crushed.title: Water pollutants
crushed.text: Sewage (pathogens), fertilisers (eutrophication), industrial chemicals, oil spills.

crushed.title: Eutrophication
crushed.text: Excess nitrates/phosphates → algae bloom → oxygen depletion → fish die.

crushed.title: Ganga Action Plan
crushed.text: India's effort to reduce sewage & industrial waste in the Ganga river.

items.label: Carbon dioxide
items.detail: Greenhouse gas — global warming. From burning fossil fuels

items.label: Carbon monoxide
items.detail: Poisonous — binds to haemoglobin. From incomplete combustion

items.label: Sulphur dioxide
items.detail: From burning coal. Causes acid rain & respiratory problems

items.label: Nitrogen oxides
items.detail: From vehicle exhaust. Acid rain + smog

items.label: CFCs
items.detail: Deplete ozone layer. Banned in most countries now

items.label: Sewage
items.detail: Organic waste + pathogens — spreads waterborne diseases

items.label: Fertilisers
items.detail: Nitrates & phosphates cause eutrophication in lakes

items.label: Industrial waste
items.detail: Toxic chemicals (mercury, lead, arsenic) poison water

items.label: Oil spills
items.detail: Coats water surface — kills aquatic life

demos.title: Acid Rain from Sulphur Dioxide
demos.description: SO₂ from burning coal dissolves in rainwater to form sulphurous acid.

steps.label: Coal burned
steps.description: Power plants burn coal releasing SO₂ into air

steps.label: Acid rain
steps.description: H₂SO₃ falls as acid rain — pH below 5.6, damages marble & metal

demos.title: Nitric Acid in Acid Rain
demos.description: Nitrogen oxides from vehicles react with water to form nitric acid.

steps.label: Vehicle exhaust
steps.description: Cars release NO₂ at high temperatures in engines

steps.label: Atmosphere
steps.description: NO₂ mixes with air and moisture

steps.label: Damage
steps.description: Acid corrodes Taj Mahal marble, kills lake fish

demos.title: Carbon Monoxide — Silent Killer
demos.description: Incomplete combustion produces CO which binds to haemoglobin 200× stronger than O₂.

steps.label: Closed room
steps.description: Coal/wood burning in poorly ventilated room

steps.label: Limited O₂
steps.description: Not enough oxygen for complete combustion

steps.label: CO builds up
steps.description: Colourless, odourless CO accumulates in air

steps.label: Haemoglobin blocked
steps.description: CO binds haemoglobin — body starved of O₂

flows.title: Eutrophication — How Fertilisers Kill a Lake
flows.description: Excess nutrients trigger an algae bloom that suffocates aquatic life.

steps.label: Farm runoff
steps.description: Rain washes nitrates & phosphates from fields into water body.

steps.label: Algae bloom
steps.description: Nutrients cause explosive algae growth on surface.

steps.label: Block sunlight
steps.description: Thick algae layer blocks light — submerged plants can't photosynthesise.

steps.label: Bacteria decompose
steps.description: Dead algae decomposed by bacteria using dissolved oxygen.

steps.label: Fish die
steps.description: Water becomes anaerobic — fish and aquatic animals suffocate.

keyTerms.definition: Rain with pH below 5.6 due to dissolved acidic gases
keyTerms.analogy: Rain that behaves like weak vinegar

keyTerms.definition: Excessive nutrient enrichment causing algae blooms and oxygen depletion
keyTerms.analogy: Overfeeding a pond until it chokes

keyTerms.definition: CO₂ traps heat in atmosphere, warming the Earth
keyTerms.analogy: A blanket of gas keeping heat from escaping
Water that won't make you sick

chapterSummary.overview: Pollution contaminates air and water, harming health and ecosystems. This chapter identifies sources (vehicles, factories, sewage), effects (acid rain, eutrophication, diseases), the greenhouse effect, ozone depletion, and measures to control pollution at individual and government levels.
keyPoints: Air pollutants: CO, CO₂, SO₂, NO₂, particulate matter, CFCs.
Greenhouse effect: CO₂ and methane trap heat → global warming and climate change.
Water pollutants: sewage, industrial waste, fertilisers — cause eutrophication and diseases.
Acid rain from SO₂ and NO₂ damages buildings, soil, and aquatic life.
Prevention: CNG vehicles, catalytic converters, STPs, rainwater harvesting, reduce-plastic use.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Explain greenhouse effect and acid rain. List water purification steps. Suggest pollution control measures.
